metadata.title: Total prosthetic replacement of the temporomandibular joint
specialty_societies: British Association of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeons
metadata.uk_national_registry: British Association of TMJ Surgeons UK register (NICE mandates submission)

nodes.label: Total prosthetic replacement of the temporomandibular joint

properties.definition: Replacement of BOTH the fossa (skull base socket) AND the mandibular condyle with prostheses
properties.distinction: Total = both components; partial/hemi = only condyle or fossa alone
incisions: Submandibular or retromandibular incision for condylar component
properties.optional_concurrent_procedure: Coronoidectomy (coronoid process removal) sometimes performed to improve post-operative mobility
properties.ukpractice: 16 surgeons submitting data to BAOMS database at time of guidance; only 5 doing >10/year

components: articular fossa (glenoid fossa of temporal bone)
properties.function: Hinge and gliding joint allowing mouth opening, closing, chewing, speech
properties.unique_features: Bilateral joint - the only bilateral joint in the body where both sides must function in concert
properties.replaced_components: Fossa (skull base) + condyle (mandible) in total replacement

properties.location: Superior process of the mandibular ramus, articulating with the temporal bone
Concavity in the temporal bone of the skull, anterior to external auditory meatus
properties.inferior_relation: Middle cranial fossa structures lie just superior
properties.risk_of_damage: Middle cranial fossa structures - only at risk after erosion from disease process (specialist adviser)

properties.location: Anterior projection of the mandibular ramus
properties.removal_indication: Coronoidectomy performed during TJR to allow greater post-operative mouth opening mobility
properties.incidence_in_evidence: 7/74 patients had coronoidectomy at time of procedure (Sidebottom 2013)

properties.function: Motor: frontalis muscle (brow elevation), corrugator supercilii, orbicularis oculi (partial)
properties.at_risk_during: Pre-auricular dissection for fossa component insertion
properties.resolution: 22/23 resolved within 6 months; 1 needed brow lift (long-term)
properties.tacit: 31% is an extremely high nerve injury rate by any surgical standard. This must be disclosed prominently in consent and should be contextualised: temporary weakness is almost universal and expected to resolve, but long-term weakness (with brow lift requirement) does occur.

nodes.label: Marginal mandibular branch of facial nerve

properties.function: Motor: depressor anguli oris, depressor labii inferioris (lower lip and mouth corner depression)
properties.at_risk_during: Submandibular incision for condylar component insertion
properties.resolution: 7/8 by 6 weeks; remaining patient by 6 months

properties.function: Sensory: skin of temporal region, external ear, and TMJ; parasympathetic relay to parotid
properties.at_risk_during: TMJ surgery - nerve passes around neck of mandible
properties.injury_incidence: Long-term (permanent) sensory loss in 14% (10/74) in Sidebottom 2013
properties.tacit: 14% permanent auriculotemporal nerve sensory loss is the most clinically significant long-term nerve complication because it is not transient. Patients experience numbness over the temporal region and anterior external ear. This is frequently underemphasised in consent compared to the more dramatic (but usually temporary) facial nerve weakness.

properties.at_risk: Bleeding during TMJ surgery can require ligation of terminal branches
properties.documented_case: 1/74 patients required neck opening to ligate terminal branches (Sidebottom 2013)
properties.clinical_consequence: Major intraoperative haemorrhage requiring escalation to cervical approach

properties.typical_age: Mean 40-47 years at time of TJR

properties.definition: Fusion of condyle to fossa - bony (true ankylosis) or fibrous (fibrous adhesions)
properties.consequence: Severely restricted or absent mouth opening
properties.prevalence_in_series: Major indication in Lobo Leandro n=300 (171/300 with ankylosis) and Loveless 2010 (all patients had documented bony or fibrous ankylosis)
properties.tacit: Ankylosis patients have often been operated on multiple times before reaching TJR. Each prior operation increases scar tissue and makes the next surgery more difficult. Mean previous operations in Loveless TJR group = 3.7. This history of multiple prior procedures is the defining feature of the TJR patient population.

properties.definition: Progressive loss of condylar bone volume
properties.consequence: Anterior open bite, retrognathia, TMJ pain, limited opening
properties.prevalence_in_series: 45/300 in Lobo Leandro; 11 had prior orthognathic surgery

stages.II: Occasional painful clicking, intermittent locking, headache
stages.III: Frequent pain, joint tenderness, headache, locking, restricted motion, painful chewing
stages.V: Variable pain, joint crepitus, painful function

properties.tjr_threshold: Classes IV and V (94% of Giannakopoulos n=288 patients were class IV or V)
properties.tacit: The TJR decision threshold is not explicit in guidance text but the evidence implies it: TJR is for Wilkes class IV-V patients who have failed all conservative and lesser surgical options. Performing TJR on class I-III patients would be inappropriate - this is a last-resort surgical intervention within an already-surgical population.

properties.description: Chronic pain from TMJ pathology - can be severe and disabling
properties.opioid_consideration: Patients on long-term opiates for pain may not be able to relinquish use post-operatively (Birmingham experience)
properties.tacit: The opioid dependency issue is crucial tacit knowledge not in the main guidance text but flagged by specialist advisers. A patient with central sensitisation from years of chronic pain may continue reporting pain even after technically successful TJR. Pre-operative opioid assessment should be part of the selection process.

properties.plateau_timing: Significant improvement ceases at ~4 years post-procedure (Lobo Leandro 10-year data)
properties.tacit: MIO plateau at 3-4 years is important prognostically. Patients who expect continued improvement after year 3 should be counselled that gains have stabilised. The plateau is not failure - it represents the mechanical limit of the reconstruction.

properties.description: Severe restriction to liquid or soft diet due to pain on chewing and limited mouth opening
properties.improvement_post_tjr: 80% of Lobo Leandro patients had general diet with no limitations at 6 months
properties.long_term: Diet scores maintained to 10-year follow-up (Lobo Leandro VAS 4.85 at 10yr vs 4.96 at 1yr)

properties.manufacturer: Biomet Microfixation (formerly Lorenz Surgical), Jacksonville, USA
properties.used_in: Lobo Leandro n=300; Giannakopoulos n=288 (442 joints)
properties.follow_up_data: Up to 10-year data (Lobo Leandro); up to 15-year international Biomet data (adviser)
properties.no_mechanical_failures: No device-related mechanical failures reported in Giannakopoulos despite complications

properties.used_in: Sidebottom n=74; Loveless n=14; Mercuri 2007 n=61 (14-year follow-up)
properties.long_term_data: 20-year data shows no failures due to UHMWPE debris (unlike knee replacements from 15yr)
properties.conflict_note: Mercuri 2007 author is Medical Consultant to TMJ Concepts

properties.manufacturer: TMJ Implants Inc. (formerly Christensen), Golden, USA
properties.used_in: Christensen 2004 retrospective + prospective data; Speculand 2009 adverse events
properties.conflict_note: Primary author of Christensen 2004 is the inventor of the prosthesis system

properties.indication: Patients with allergy to standard cobalt-chromium metal components
properties.evidence: Hussain 2014 (n=55, 1yr): outcomes similarly favourable to non-allergic patients with standard prosthesis; no hypersensitivity reactions; no failures
properties.patch_testing: Patch testing is recommended/commonly done to guide prosthesis choice (Committee comment 6.2)
properties.tacit: Metal allergy testing is a pre-operative selection criterion that is easily overlooked. If a patient is implanted with cobalt-chromium without patch testing and develops hypersensitivity, the implant may require removal. Testing is far cheaper than revision surgery.

properties.failure_mechanism: Debris and foreign-body giant cell reaction within joint
properties.clinical_relevance: Some patients may still carry these implants; TJR revision is common indication in previously implanted patients
properties.prevalence_in_evidence: 46% of Mercuri 2007 patients had previous Proplast-Teflon implant

properties.definition: Surgical removal of the coronoid process of the mandible
properties.purpose: Removes restriction to mouth opening from tight temporalis muscle; allows greater post-operative mobility
properties.incidence: 7/74 patients in Sidebottom 2013 had coronoidectomy at time of TMJ TJR
properties.indication: Severe ankylosis or restricted opening where coronoid is expected to limit mobility gain
properties.tacit: Coronoidectomy decision is made intraoperatively based on achieved mouth opening with the prosthesis in situ. If MIO under general anaesthesia is still limited with prosthesis placed, coronoidectomy is added. This intraoperative flexibility is not described in the guidance text.

properties.success_rate: Successful long-term in approximately one-third of patients (specialist adviser)
properties.reserved_for: Children - because it has growth capacity
properties.current_status: Still standard practice in developing countries; previously standard in all settings
properties.tacit: The specialist adviser's explicit statement that the only real alternative to alloplastic TJR is costochondral graft with ~33% long-term success is important context for patient selection. A patient who understands that the biological alternative succeeds in only 1/3 of cases long-term will have a very different risk-benefit perception of TJR.

properties.definition: Creation of joint space by condylectomy/fossa remodelling with interposition of a biological or alloplastic material
properties.comparator_to_tjr: Loveless 2010 compared TJR to IA in ankylosis patients
properties.mio_outcome: MIO change: TJR +9.4mm vs IA +18mm (p=0.02 unadjusted; NS after adjustment)
properties.patient_group: IA patients had fewer prior operations (mean 1.1 vs 3.7 for TJR) - important baseline difference
properties.tacit: The Loveless comparison is heavily confounded: TJR group had 3.7x more previous operations. The unadjusted result favouring IA becomes non-significant after adjustment. Comparing outcomes between groups with fundamentally different prior surgery histories is problematic. TJR is typically the later intervention in heavily-operated patients.

nodes.label: Conservative management (rest, NSAIDs, bite splints, physiotherapy)

properties.failure_required: TJR only considered when alternative treatments have failed (NICE 3.1)

properties.indication: Early-to-mid stage TMJ disorders; before open surgery

nodes.label: Partial TMJ replacement (disc, fossa, or condyle alone)

properties.distinction: Replaces only ONE component (disc, fossa, or condyle) rather than both
properties.excluded_from_htg352: HTG352 covers only TOTAL replacement (both components)
properties.position_in_ladder: After conservative and arthroscopic failure; before total joint replacement

properties.resolution: 22/23 resolved within 6 months; 1 required brow lift (long-term)
properties.mechanism: Traction or stretch injury during pre-auricular dissection
properties.clinical_appearance: Inability to raise eyebrow on affected side; brow ptosis
properties.long_term_consequence: 1/74 (1.4%) required brow lift procedure for permanent weakness
properties.tacit: 31% is one of the highest nerve injury rates in elective surgery. This must be disclosed but contextualised: 96% of cases resolve spontaneously within 6 months. The brow lift requirement (1/74) is a useful concrete consequence to explain to patients about the permanent risk.

properties.mechanism: Stretch or compression during submandibular dissection
properties.clinical_appearance: Weakness of lower lip depression; asymmetric smile

properties.mechanism: More extensive nerve traction or compression

properties.territory: Distribution of auriculotemporal nerve: skin of temporal region and anterior external ear
properties.tacit: This is the most commonly under-discussed TMJ TJR complication. 14% permanent numbness in a sensory territory patients notice daily (hairbrushing, touching the side of the face). Surgeons may emphasise the impressive pain relief and focus attention away from this smaller but permanent deficit. Proactive, clear consent disclosure is essential.

properties.mercuri_2011: 1.5% of joints (51/3368); mean onset 6 months (range 2 weeks to 12 years)
properties.sidebottom_2013: 3% (2/74): periapical dental infection → biofilm; otitis externa + cellulitis + biofilm
properties.consequence_of_infection: Device removal and replacement (both Sidebottom cases successfully revised)
properties.revision_success: Successful revision with new prosthesis in both Sidebottom cases
properties.improving_over_time: Giannakopoulos: infection rate decreased as procedure duration shortened, instrumentation improved, infection control became more stringent
properties.antibiotic_guidelines: Mercuri 2011 developed preliminary perioperative/postoperative/prophylactic antibiotic guidelines
properties.tacit: Infection onset up to 12 years post-implantation (Mercuri 2011) means lifelong vigilance is required. Dental procedures are a potential haematogenous seeding risk. This is analogous to hip/knee arthroplasty dental prophylaxis protocols, yet no formal NICE recommendation on this is made.

properties.sidebottom_detail: 4/5 recognised on operating table; 1 developed within hours post-op
properties.management: Light elastic intermaxillary fixation for 1 week
properties.outcome: None recurred; all had successful outcomes at 1 year
properties.tacit: Dislocation management with 1-week elastic IMF is specific procedural knowledge. The difference between intraoperative and early post-operative dislocation management is the same: elastic IMF. The key tacit point is that recognition on the operating table allows immediate correction; post-operative dislocation within hours still has the same benign outcome with conservative management.

properties.incidence: <1% (11/2106) in Speculand 2009; contributes to 3.2% implant removal rate in Giannakopoulos
properties.consequence: Can restrict joint mobility; may require implant removal
properties.mechanism: Aberrant bone formation in soft tissues around implant

properties.severe_case: 1 patient required neck opening to ligate terminal branches of external carotid artery
properties.tacit: The carotid artery ligation case illustrates that TMJ surgery can rapidly escalate to life-threatening haemorrhage. This reflects proximity to major head and neck vasculature. Preoperative imaging of local vasculature and clear surgical planning of vessel exposure is essential.

Post-operative malocclusion after TMJ TJR may be structural (implant position) or neuromuscular (muscle rebalancing as pain resolves). Distinguishing early is important: structural malocclusion may need revision; neuromuscular malocclusion often self-corrects with jaw exercises.

properties.mechanism: Aberrant regeneration of auriculotemporal nerve fibres - parasympathetic fibres reinnervate sweat glands instead of parotid
properties.clinical_presentation: Sweating of pre-auricular skin during eating or anticipation of food

properties.mechanism: External auditory meatus proximity; eustachian tube dysfunction
properties.tacit: Proximity of the TMJ to the external ear canal and middle ear means even non-catastrophic surgical manipulation can alter eustachian tube function and middle ear pressure. Ear examination should be part of pre-operative and post-operative assessment.

properties.evidence: Mercuri 2007: each additional prior operation increases pain by 0.63 units (95% CI 0.04-1.2) and function by 0.68 units
properties.loveless_group_difference: TJR group had mean 3.7 prior operations vs 1.1 for IA group - key confounder
properties.tacit: Prior operations are the most important negative prognostic factor. A patient with 5+ previous failed procedures on the same joint has a fundamentally worse prognosis than a patient having TJR as primary joint surgery. This must be incorporated into pre-operative counselling about expected outcomes.

nodes.label: Metal allergy as prosthesis selection criterion

properties.committee_comment: NICE Committee 6.2: patients with metal allergies may react to certain prostheses; patch testing commonly done to guide choice
properties.evidence_for_all_ti: Hussain 2014 (n=55): all-titanium outcomes equally favourable at 1 year; no hypersensitivity reactions
properties.tacit: Patch testing is a pre-operative selection step that determines prosthesis type, not whether to proceed with surgery. Omitting patch testing risks implanting a cobalt-chromium prosthesis in an allergic patient, requiring later revision. This should be standard pre-operative workup.

nodes.label: Specialist unit and regular practice requirement

properties.nice_1_2: Patient selection in specialist units by team with regular practice and specific expertise
properties.uk_reality: 16 surgeons submit to BAOMS database; only 5 do >10/yr
properties.controversy: Procedure was controversially introduced in UK by representative to any willing surgeon (specialist adviser)
properties.cost_issue: Cost not met within standard procedural costs; national centres of excellence with appropriate funding needed
properties.tacit: The specialist adviser's warning about 'overuse by inadequately trained surgeons' and 'controversial introduction to any surgeon who would like to try one' reflects a real-world governance failure at the time of guidance. The NICE requirement for specialist unit and regular practice is a direct response to this. A surgeon who performs TJR once per year is unlikely to maintain the skill level required.

properties.nice_1_4: Clinicians should submit details on all patients to the British Association of TMJ Surgeons UK register
properties.purpose: Long-term safety and efficacy data on various prostheses
properties.rationale: Range of prostheses available that may differ in long-term safety and efficacy (Committee 6.1)
properties.uk_baseline_data: Idle 2014 (n=402): UK TMJ replacement database baseline report - 3 primary systems (TMJ Concepts, Biomet, Christensen)
properties.tacit: Registry submission is mandatory, not optional. Prosthesis-specific long-term data is essential because the Committee explicitly acknowledged that different prostheses may have different long-term profiles. Without systematic registry data, individual surgeons cannot know whether their prosthesis is performing appropriately compared to benchmarks.

properties.nice_1_3: Procedure only by clinicians with specific training AND experience
training_components: Basic training in anatomical cadaveric workshop
Initial cases supervised by recognised experienced surgeon
Good experience in ALL forms of TMJ surgery (not just total replacement)
properties.tacit: The cadaveric workshop and supervised initial cases requirement is important tacit guidance not quantified in NICE text. A surgeon who has not done this specific surgical simulation before their first live case is operating without foundational training. The complexity of the surgical approaches (pre-auricular + submandibular) and proximity to facial nerve and major vessels makes this non-negotiable.

properties.nice_1_2: Patient selection should include consideration of all relevant medical and surgical options; BAOMS has produced guidelines
giannakopoulos_explicit_criteria: Clinical and imaging evidence consistent with anatomic joint pathology
Previous failure of non-surgical treatment or failed implant
High probability of improvement from surgical treatment
No serious compromising general medical conditions
exclusion_criteria: Insufficient bone quantity or quality to support device

properties.authors: Leandro LF, Ono HY, Loureiro CC et al.
properties.title: A ten-year experience and follow-up of three hundred patients fitted with the Biomet/Lorenz Microfixation TMJ replacement system
properties.journal: International Journal of Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery
properties.key_positive: No infections, no prosthetic system removals, no loosened screw adjustments at any follow-up

properties.authors: Giannakopoulos HE, Sinn DP, Quinn PD
properties.title: Biomet Microfixation Temporomandibular Joint Replacement System: a 3-year follow-up study
properties.conflict_of_interest: Statistical analysis provided by Director of Research at Biomet

properties.title: One year prospective outcome analysis and complications following total replacement of the temporomandibular joint with the TMJ Concepts system
properties.journal: British Journal of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery
properties.key_value: Only UK single-centre prospective study; most detailed nerve complication data

properties.title: Current status of replacement of the temporomandibular joint in the United Kingdom
properties.journal: British Journal of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery
properties.study_type: Review of current practice with manufacturer adverse event data
properties.key_value: Largest adverse events dataset; 20-year UK experience review

properties.title: Perioperative, postoperative, and prophylactic use of antibiotics in alloplastic total temporomandibular joint replacement surgery: a survey and preliminary guidelines
properties.key_finding: 1.5% infected joints; 62.7% of infected joints required removal/replacement; preliminary antibiotic guidelines

properties.authors: Loveless TP, Bjornland T, Dodson TB et al.
properties.title: Efficacy of temporomandibular joint ankylosis surgical treatment
properties.key_limitation: TJR group had 3.7x more prior operations - major confounder; groups not comparable at baseline

properties.authors: Mercuri LG, Edibam NR, Giobbie-Hurder A
properties.title: Fourteen-year follow-up of a patient-fitted total temporomandibular joint reconstruction system
properties.conflict_of_interest: Author is Medical Consultant to TMJ Concepts
properties.key_predictors: Prior operations increase pain; each additional operation adds 0.63 pain units; age weakly predicts poor QoL

properties.authors: Christensen RW, Alexander R, Curry JT et al.
properties.title: Hemi and total TMJ reconstruction using the Christensen prostheses: a retrospective and prospective evaluation
properties.conflict_of_interest: Primary author is inventor of the Christensen joint system
properties.key_limitation: Mixed total and partial replacement data not always separated

properties.event: 2011: FDA ordered 3 manufacturers (TMJ Solutions, TMJ Medical, Biomet Microfixation) to conduct post-market surveillance studies
properties.trigger: FDA analysis showed substantial number of patients with implants replaced within 3 years of implantation because of extreme pain
properties.significance: 3-year replacement rate considerably shorter than expected minimum 5-year device lifespan based on premarket mechanical testing
properties.tacit: The FDA order is a significant regulatory signal that performance in clinical practice diverges from bench testing predictions. Short-term replacement due to pain (not mechanical failure) suggests central pain sensitisation in some patients - the implant works mechanically but the patient still has pain. This is the same phenomenon the Birmingham adviser noted about opioid-dependent patients.

properties.committee_6_1: Technique and materials have been developing for a long time and may continue to evolve; range of prostheses available that may differ in long-term safety and efficacy
properties.obsolete_risks: Proplast-Teflon: debris + foreign body giant cell reaction; Silastic: mechanical failure
properties.current_longevity: Biomet: up to 15-year data; TMJ Concepts: 20-year data shows no UHMWPE debris failures
properties.tacit: The history of TMJ implant failures (Proplast-Teflon, Silastic) should inform surgeon humility about current systems. A prosthesis that performs well at 5 years may have unforeseen problems at 15-20 years. Registry submission (NICE 1.4) is the mechanism for detecting late failures before they become a widespread problem.

edges.label: TMJ disorder manifests as chronic pain
TMJ disorder manifests as limited mouth opening (MIO)
TMJ disorder manifests as dietary restriction from pain on chewing
TMJ ankylosis is a subtype of TMJ disorder requiring TJR
Condylar resorption is a cause of TMJ disorder requiring TJR
TMJ disorder is first managed conservatively
Failed conservative management escalates to arthroscopic surgery

properties.trigger: Persistent pain or limited opening despite adequate conservative trial

edges.label: Failed arthroscopic management escalates to partial joint replacement options
Failed partial replacement or exhausted all options leads to total joint replacement

properties.condition: Alternative treatments must have failed before TJR is considered

edges.label: Failed interpositional arthroplasty is a common pathway to TJR

properties.tacit_note: Loveless 2010: TJR patients had mean 3.7 prior operations vs IA 1.1; TJR is for the more severely-operated group

edges.label: TJR treats TMJ pain with dramatic and sustained improvement
TJR treats limited mouth opening

properties.mio_gain: 11.3→38.9mm at 1yr (Lobo Leandro); significant gains until year 3-4, then plateau
properties.plateau_timing: MIO improvement becomes non-significant after year 4

edges.label: TJR resolves dietary restriction: 80% on general diet without limitations at 6 months
TJR risks temporal branch facial nerve weakness in 31% of patients
TJR risks marginal mandibular palsy in 11% of patients
TJR risks permanent auriculotemporal nerve sensory loss in 14% of patients

properties.tacit_note: Most commonly under-emphasised consent item due to non-dramatic nature

edges.label: TJR risks peri-implant infection in 1.5-3% of patients
TJR risks prosthesis dislocation
TJR risks major intraoperative haemorrhage requiring transfusion
TJR risks heterotopic bone formation
TJR risks malocclusion or prosthesis malposition
TJR carries anecdotal risk of Frey's syndrome
TJR carries anecdotal risk of hearing changes
Infection may require prosthesis removal and revision

properties.revision_outcome: Both Sidebottom cases revised successfully with new prosthesis
properties.tacit_note: Revision after infection is feasible and successful; infection is not irreversible

edges.label: Prosthesis dislocation managed with 1-week elastic intermaxillary fixation

properties.outcome: All 5 Sidebottom cases resolved without recurrence

edges.label: Metal allergy determines use of all-titanium prosthesis

properties.testing: Patch testing recommended before implant selection

edges.label: Metal allergy screening modulates prosthesis selection but does not preclude TJR
Each prior operation worsens post-operative pain prognosis by 0.63 VAS units

properties.implication: Heavily operated patients should be counselled for more modest pain expectations

edges.label: Wilkes class IV-V is the de facto indication threshold for TJR

properties.evidence: 94% of Giannakopoulos patients were Wilkes IV or V
properties.tacit_note: Not stated explicitly in guidance but derivable from patient populations

edges.label: Specialist unit requirement governs who may offer TJR
Specific training and experience requirement governs who may perform TJR
Registry submission is mandated for all TJR patients
BAOMS patient selection guidelines define eligibility for TJR
Costochondral graft is the only biological alternative to alloplastic TJR

properties.tacit_note: 33% long-term success of the only biological alternative is the implicit benchmark that makes alloplastic TJR compelling

edges.label: TJR may include concurrent coronoidectomy to improve mouth opening

properties.decision: Intraoperative based on achieved MIO with prosthesis in situ

edges.label: Lobo Leandro 2013 provides strongest efficacy evidence (n=300, 10-year data)
Giannakopoulos 2012 documents improving infection rates over time with better technique
Sidebottom 2013 documents most detailed nerve complication data
Speculand 2009 documents adverse events across 2620-patient UK database
Mercuri 2011 establishes preliminary antibiotic prophylaxis guidelines for TMJ TJR
Mercuri 2007 quantifies impact of prior operations on outcome (0.63 pain units per operation)
FDA surveillance order highlights gap between bench testing and clinical performance

properties.tacit_note: Short-term replacement due to pain rather than mechanical failure suggests patient selection issue, not purely device failure

edges.label: Prosthesis diversity and evolution justifies mandatory registry submission for long-term comparison
TMJ contains the mandibular condyle component
TMJ contains the articular fossa (skull base) component
TJR replaces the mandibular condyle with prosthesis
TJR replaces the articular fossa with prosthesis
Pre-auricular dissection injures temporal branch causing weakness
TMJ surgery injures auriculotemporal nerve causing permanent sensory loss
External carotid terminal branches are source of major intraoperative haemorrhage
Proplast-Teflon implant failure creates new/worse TMJ disorder requiring revision TJR
TJR may use custom TMJ Concepts prosthesis
TJR may use stock Biomet/Lorenz prosthesis
TJR may use Christensen/TMJ Implants system
TJR may use all-titanium prosthesis when metal allergy identified
MIO improvement plateaus at 3-4 years with no significant further gains

properties.evidence: Lobo Leandro: statistically significant improvement only up to year 3; NS thereafter to 10 years
properties.tacit_note: This plateau is clinical knowledge that should be used to set post-operative expectations

key_clinical_reasoning_chains.name: Temporal branch nerve weakness as expected, not exceptional
key_clinical_reasoning_chains.description: 31% temporal branch weakness rate is almost universal in TMJ TJR and should be presented to patients as an expected temporary effect, not as a complication. 96% resolve in 6 months. The permanent rate (1.4%) is the figure requiring emphatic consent discussion. Framing matters: 'nearly everyone gets temporary brow weakness' vs 'there is a nerve injury risk' convey very different information.

key_clinical_reasoning_chains.name: Auriculotemporal sensory loss - the under-disclosed permanent complication
key_clinical_reasoning_chains.description: 14% permanent auriculotemporal sensory loss is consistently under-emphasised in consent discussions because numbness is less dramatic than weakness. However, it affects daily activities (touching the face, hairbrushing) and is permanent. Proactive, specific consent documentation for this permanent deficit is essential.

key_clinical_reasoning_chains.name: Prior operations as the dominant outcome predictor
key_clinical_reasoning_chains.description: Each prior TMJ operation independently increases post-operative pain by 0.63 VAS units (Mercuri 2007). A patient with 5 prior operations should expect 3+ units less pain relief than a patient with no prior surgery. This calibration of outcome expectations based on surgical history is not in the guidance text but is the most important patient-specific prognostic factor.

key_clinical_reasoning_chains.name: Infection: chronic risk requiring lifelong vigilance
key_clinical_reasoning_chains.description: Infection onset up to 12 years post-implantation (Mercuri 2011) means patients carry a permanent low-level infection risk analogous to hip/knee arthroplasty. Dental procedures create haematogenous seeding risk. Despite no formal NICE antibiotic prophylaxis recommendation, the clinical parallel to orthopaedic implant protocols is strong and should inform clinical practice.

key_clinical_reasoning_chains.name: Volume-outcome relationship and the 5 cases/year threshold
key_clinical_reasoning_chains.description: The NICE requirement for specialist unit and regular practice is reinforced by adviser warnings about 'overuse by inadequately trained surgeons' and controversial early introduction to any willing surgeon. The suggested minimum of 5 cases/year, only 16 UK submitters, and only 5 doing >10/year paint a picture of a highly concentrated procedure. Surgeons below this threshold should refer rather than operate.

key_clinical_reasoning_chains.name: Opioid dependency as a relative contraindication
key_clinical_reasoning_chains.description: Patients on long-term opiates for TMJ pain may not relinquish opiate use after successful TJR (Birmingham experience). Central sensitisation from years of chronic pain means the analgesic benefit may not translate to opiate discontinuation. This should be assessed pre-operatively and forms part of the BAOMS patient selection process.

key_clinical_reasoning_chains.name: MIO plateau as outcome calibration
key_clinical_reasoning_chains.description: MIO improvement is significant up to year 3-4 then plateaus permanently (Lobo Leandro 10-year data). Patients who attend follow-up at year 4+ expecting continued improvement should be counselled that their current opening represents the long-term result. Continued physiotherapy to maintain (not improve) opening is the goal after year 3.
